hero.label: Digital Portfolio
hero.role: AI Algorithm Engineer · Former Competitive Athlete
hero.tagline: Bringing an athlete's resilience to the world of code and algorithms—swinging with full commitment.

about.label: About Me
about.title: About Me
about.p1: I'm Yung-Ling Chu, a developer who transitioned from competitive badminton to AI algorithm engineering. Countless matches and recovery cycles on court built exceptional mental toughness and an uncompromising drive to tackle hard problems.
about.p2: After retiring from competition, I brought the same sense of rhythm and discipline into programming and algorithms—from model training and system deployment to front-end delivery, I start by clarifying the problem, then advance step by step in verifiable increments.
about.p3: To me, coding is like competition: every iteration is a rally, with the goal of executing consistently under pressure and continuously improving.
about.quote: "Competition taught me that results come from focused practice in every rep. Writing code is no different—break complex problems down, then rally back one shot at a time."

aboutBio.name: 朱詠翎

contact.license: Ordinary heavy motorcycle, Ordinary light vehicle

education.detail: Department of Computer Science and Information Engineering

aboutBio.experienceSummary: Contributed to computer-vision and AI projects covering image recognition, system testing, on-site application observation, and technical feedback. Later transferred internally to Business Development after growing interest in product applications, customer needs, and business models—helping organize market and partner requirements, plan product/service offerings, and translate client feedback into actionable directions for internal teams.

ai.title: AI Algorithm Engineer
items: Worked on computer-vision projects including image-based detection, pose estimation, system testing, and real-world application validation.
Applied CS and AI knowledge to understand technical logic, analyze field scenarios, and identify practical issues during system deployment.
Organized technical issues and user feedback to support model/system improvement and strengthen real-world applicability.

bd.title: Business Development
items: Ideated and advanced a new sports-tech application initiative, contributing to concept planning, partnership-model design, and execution direction.
Collaborated with engineering, UI/UX, and digital media teams to align technical feasibility, user experience, and visual presentation needs.
Visited 10+ sports venues and school partners, building cooperation interest and opening potential channels for follow-on projects.
Supported school-oriented project development including proposal preparation, communication follow-ups, requirement clarification, and internal task coordination.
Helped plan tech-enabled winter/summer camp programs, shaping learning activities, student experience flows, and project deliverables.
Joined semester-based PBL programs with partner schools, assisting curriculum planning, project timeline management, and coordination between school needs and internal execution.

projects.label: Selected Projects
projects.title: Selected Projects
projects.description: From competitive mindset to algorithm implementation—these are three areas I've been focused on recently.

badmintonDefenseAnalysis.title: AI-Driven Badminton Defense Analysis using Pose Estimation
badmintonDefenseAnalysis.description: Combining MediaPipe pose estimation with a VICON segmental body-mass model to compute whole-body center-of-mass offset (CoM offset), and applying outlier filtering with temporal continuity to build an objective, interpretable badminton defensive gap detection system.

algoMatchDashboard.title: AlgoMatch Dashboard
algoMatchDashboard.description: Real-time monitoring of model training metrics and resource utilization in an engineer-friendly observability interface.

pressuredDecisionLab.title: Pressured Decision Lab
pressuredDecisionLab.description: A lightweight experimental platform simulating high-pressure decision scenarios to explore how resilience strategies affect performance.
